steps.title: Fit line k from the graph
steps.content: Use the line that is actually shown. Line $k$ passes through $(0,4)$ and $(3,0)$, so its slope is $$\frac{0-4}{3-0}=-\frac{4}{3}.$$

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals. Since line $k$ has slope $-\frac{4}{3}$, line $j$ has slope $\frac{3}{4}$.
